title: MCP Lifecycle Management and Metadata Cache

tasks.title: Create MCP server manager
tasks.description: Add a central manager keyed by server name and config hash, with tracked connection state, timestamps, errors, and cleanup handles.

tasks.title: Make manager concurrency-safe
tasks.description: Ensure concurrent calls for the same disconnected server share a single startup attempt.

tasks.title: Implement lazy startup rules
tasks.description: Start servers only for actions that need live connections, using cache where possible and bounding startup failures.

tasks.title: Implement idle cleanup
tasks.description: Use settings.idleTimeout, close SDK clients gracefully, terminate owned child processes, and avoid stopping active calls.

tasks.title: Add MCP metadata cache file
tasks.description: Persist tool metadata to /data/canvas-agent/mcp-cache.json with config hashes, schemas, descriptions, timestamps, and safe permissions.

tasks.title: Implement cache invalidation
tasks.description: Invalidate cached metadata when mcp.json changes, server config hashes change, TTL expires, or live tool lists differ.

tasks.title: Expose status observability
tasks.description: Return config path, hash, server states, active calls, cache age, and last errors from mcp status without exposing secret env values.

tasks.title: Add best-effort shutdown cleanup
tasks.description: Clean up live MCP clients where runtime shutdown hooks are available without depending on one global process lifetime.

tasks.title: Add lifecycle and cache tests
tasks.description: Test concurrent startup, idle close, cache write/reuse, config hash invalidation, and failed startup backoff.

tasks.title: Verify Step 3 implementation
tasks.description: Run npm build and only perform UI tests if visible Settings or status UI changes are introduced.
